reason: D1-D5 decisions, 7 MVP blocks (M1-M7), 10 acceptance criteria, scope guard.
Module boundary, data contracts (CooccurrenceMatrix, SpikeResult, TagBoostInfo), algorithm pseudo-code, integration points, failure semantics, rollback shape.
Ordered 8-step checklist with per-step validation commands, review gates, rollback points, workload estimate.
Verbatim phi(pos,n) formula, direction convention, persistence + rebuild lifecycle from VCPToolBox source. Implementation must match line-by-line.
Full V6 spike propagation algorithm walkthrough; the implementation of propagate() must mirror this.
Module placement decisions, data structure tradeoffs, lifecycle wiring concrete locations.
Edge cases the source handles + parameter defaults; AC drafts.
Test layering, deterministic-output expectation, forbidden patterns. Spike algorithm and matrix builder both must follow.
SQL conventions for the cooccurrence builder reading manual_tags; FK CASCADE expectations from Phase 0 still apply.
ServiceError code conventions for new errors. Phase 1 uses graceful-degrade by default but should still standardize codes.
Low-cardinality label rules for the 3 new Prometheus metrics; structured log fields for skipped reasons in TagBoostInfo.
Module placement (tag_cooccurrence.py, wave_tag_spike.py) + data assets (data/_global/tag_cooccurrence/) follow the canonical layout.
Phase 1 spans tag_rebuild → state.RebuildTask → api response → search_runtime → wave_searcher → metrics; cross-layer consistency required.
Reuse atomic-write helpers, iter_canonical_tags_with_vectors, EPA project; avoid duplicating cosine helpers.
